title: viola pallens subsp. hultenii
intro: Viola pallens subsp. hultenii, also known as Arctic pansy or Hulten's pansy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the viola genus, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of Viola pallens subsp. hultenii have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which may help to soothe and heal the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair. Herbal preparations of Viola pallens subsp. hultenii can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, and may be used topically as a cream or salve to treat skin conditions, or taken internally as a tea to promote overall health and well-being.
